The patient with airway obstruction (option D) should be prioritized as they have an immediate life-threatening condition that requires urgent intervention. This patient cannot receive proper care without first ensuring their airway is clear, making them the highest priority for shifting immediately to the hospital. Answer: **D**. 

The other patients also require significant attention but can potentially wait until after stabilizing or clearing the airway if time allows. For instance, circulation shock (option C), head injuries (option B), and severe hemorrhages with fractures (option A) all necessitate prompt treatment but may not pose an imminent threat compared to respiratory failure due to obstructed breathing.
